skill.name: aippt
skill.description: Create template-based AI presentations by exporting slide images, analyzing layouts, mapping content, generating images, and packaging them into PPTX files.
skill.summary: A guided workflow for turning source content and a presentation template into AI-generated PPTX decks.
skill.author: Ceeon

security_audit.summary: Static analysis found many command, network, credential, filesystem, and obfuscation indicators. Most backtick, weak-crypto, and entropy alerts are false positives from Markdown code fences, Chinese text, and examples, but the skill does intentionally upload local slide images to third-party hosts and call external image APIs with bearer keys. No prompt injection or confirmed malicious intent was found, so this is high risk rather than blocked.

high_findings.title: Local Slide Images Uploaded to Third-Party Hosts
high_findings.description: The workflow instructs users to upload local PPT template images to public image hosting services before image generation. This can expose confidential presentation content, branding, customer data, or internal strategy slides to third parties.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands explicitly send local file paths to external upload endpoints. This is a confirmed data disclosure risk, although it appears to be part of the documented workflow rather than hidden exfiltration.

high_findings.title: Hardcoded Image Host API Key in Documentation
high_findings.description: The upload instructions include a concrete freeimage.host API key in the URL. Even if this is intended as a shared example key, publishing reusable credentials encourages unsafe credential handling and may allow abuse or unexpected attribution.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The key value is visible in the documented curl command. I cannot confirm whether it is active, but hardcoded third-party keys are a strong security concern.

high_findings.title: Credentialed External Image Generation API Calls
high_findings.description: The skill directs users to send prompts and uploaded image URLs to external image generation APIs using bearer tokens. This is legitimate for the skill purpose, but it creates privacy and credential exposure risk if users process sensitive slides.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The documentation clearly shows Authorization bearer headers and a secrets template. The risk is confirmed, but no evidence shows secret theft or covert network destinations.

medium_findings.title: External Command Workflow Requires User Review
medium_findings.description: The skill provides shell, PowerShell, curl, npm, and Node commands for exporting, uploading, generating, downloading, and packaging slides. These commands are visible and mostly legitimate, but users should review paths, endpoints, and installed packages before execution.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are real and user-executed, but I did not find hidden dynamic command construction or an automatic execution path in the skill files.

medium_findings.title: Local Filesystem Script Reads Images and Writes PPTX Output
medium_findings.description: The bundled Node script resolves a user-provided image directory, reads image filenames, embeds matching images, and writes the PPTX output into that directory. This is expected behavior for a PPT packaging tool, but it should be run only on intended folders.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The filesystem reads and output write are directly visible in the script. The behavior is bounded to the user-supplied directory and no network calls are present.

low_findings.title: Static Backtick, Weak Crypto, and Entropy Alerts Mostly Dismissed
low_findings.description: Many static findings are caused by Markdown code fences, inline examples, Chinese prose, and formatting rather than Ruby backtick execution, weak cryptography, or obfuscated payloads. No evidence found of binary blobs, encrypted content, or prompt injection text in the reviewed files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review shows these locations are documentation blocks or prose. This strongly supports false-positive classification for the scanner patterns, while preserving the separate real network and credential risks.

dangerous_patterns.title: curl Uploads Local Files to External Services
dangerous_patterns.description: Documented curl commands post local image files to external image hosting services to obtain public URLs.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands use multipart file upload fields with local file path placeholders and external URLs.

dangerous_patterns.title: Bearer Token Sent to External Image API
dangerous_patterns.description: The API examples send an Authorization bearer token to external image generation endpoints.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The Authorization header is explicit in the examples. This is normal API use but security-sensitive.

dangerous_patterns.title: Node Script Processes Local Filesystem Paths
dangerous_patterns.description: The PPTX packaging script resolves input paths, reads a directory, embeds image files, and writes an output PPTX.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The script performs local filesystem operations by design and does not appear obfuscated.

content.value_statement: Creating many PPT slides from source content is slow when templates must stay consistent. This skill provides a structured workflow for template analysis, content mapping, AI image generation, and PPTX packaging.
actual_capabilities: Guides users to export PPT templates as slide images for AI reference.
Defines a method for analyzing template layouts and reusable slide structures.
Helps split source content into a planned sequence of presentation pages.
Maps each page to a template layout or creative slide format with prompts.
Documents image upload and external image generation steps for slide images.
Includes a Node script that packages local images into a 16:9 PPTX file.
limitations: Requires external tools such as PowerPoint, WPS, Keynote, poppler, Node.js, or image APIs.
May expose sensitive slide images when using public image hosting services.
AI-generated text in images may contain errors, especially for Chinese text.
Does not provide a complete offline generation pipeline or built-in API client.

use_cases.title: Marketing Deck Refresh
use_cases.description: Turn a campaign brief into a new presentation while preserving an existing brand template.

use_cases.title: Training Material Conversion
use_cases.description: Convert long-form training notes into structured slide pages with matching visual prompts.

use_cases.title: Consulting Proposal Draft
use_cases.description: Map client research into a professional deck structure before generating slide images and a PPTX.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Deck Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the AIPPT workflow to turn this content into a 12-slide presentation plan. Keep one main idea per slide and identify the slide type for each page.

prompt_templates.title: Analyze a Template
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request after exporting template images.
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze these exported template images and create a reusable layout library. Mark which areas must stay unchanged and which text areas can be replaced.

prompt_templates.title: Map Content to Layouts
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request for matching content to a template.
prompt_templates.prompt: Map my page plan to the template layout library. For each slide, choose the best layout or explain why a creative page is needed.

prompt_templates.title: Generate Production Prompts
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for image generation preparation.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create final image-generation prompts for each slide using the AIPPT method. Include reference image URLs, must-keep style constraints, exact content, and a review checklist.

output_examples.input: A 2,000-word product launch brief and an existing brand presentation template.
output: A slide-by-slide page plan with cover, agenda, section pages, content slides, and closing page.
A layout mapping table that matches each page to a template image.
Generation-ready prompts that preserve brand colors and layout rules.

output_examples.input: A folder of generated slide images ready to assemble into a deck.
output_examples.output: A 16:9 PPTX file where each image becomes one full-slide page in numeric filename order.

output_examples.input: A training article that needs a more visual presentation format.
output: A concise content segmentation plan with one teaching point per slide.
Recommendations for lists, timelines, comparison slides, and quote slides.
Notes about which slides should avoid AI-rendered text.

best_practices: Use sanitized template images when slides contain confidential or customer data.
Review every generated slide for text accuracy, brand compliance, and layout drift.
Keep API keys outside shared repositories and replace example keys before production use.
anti_patterns: Uploading confidential templates or internal strategy slides to public image hosts.
Expecting AI image generation to render complex Chinese text perfectly on the first try.
Running shell commands or npm installs without reviewing paths, endpoints, and package sources.

faq.question: Does this skill create native editable PowerPoint objects?
faq.answer: No. The included script packages generated images as full-slide images inside a PPTX file.

faq.question: Can I use it without external image APIs?
faq.answer: You can use the planning and template analysis methods, but image generation requires an external or separate image model.

faq.question: Is it safe for confidential presentations?
faq.answer: Use caution. The documented workflow uploads local images to third-party hosts, which may expose sensitive content.

faq.question: Which AI tools are supported?
faq.answer: The report lists Claude, Codex, and Claude Code as supported tools for using the workflow instructions.

faq.question: What inputs should I prepare?
faq.answer: Prepare source content, a PPT template, exported template images, and access to any required image generation service.

faq.question: Why are generated slide words sometimes wrong?
faq.answer: Image models can distort text. The skill recommends simple wording, exact quoted text, and manual review.
